metadata.title: Rumor Tracker & Fact Check
metadata.description: Analyzing rumors and verifying trending claims about the Iran-Israel conflict. Each entry is independently researched with linked sources.
metadata.methodology: Claims are sourced from trending social media posts, news outlets, and government statements. Each is independently checked against multiple sources. Verdicts: Confirmed (verified true), Not Likely (available evidence contradicts), Likely (evidence supports but not fully confirmed), Unlikely (evidence contradicts but remains inconclusive), Unverified (insufficient evidence either way).

claims.claim: Benjamin Netanyahu has been killed in an Iranian missile strike
claims.summary: Viral claims that Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu was killed in an Iranian strike have flooded social media since March 12. The rumors are fueled by three things: (1) a video where users claimed Netanyahu had 'six fingers' — suggesting AI deepfake, (2) a fabricated screenshot of the Israeli PM's X account announcing his death, and (3) his son Yair's absence from social media since March 9. Multiple independent fact-checkers have rated the death claims FALSE. Snopes confirmed the death screenshot was fabricated. PolitiFact rated the 'six fingers' claim 'Pants on Fire' — the apparent sixth finger was a shadow from his palm muscle (hypothenar eminence). AI detection tools found only 0.1% likelihood the video was AI-generated. The video was published directly by the Israeli Government Press Office. Netanyahu's last confirmed live appearance was a press conference on March 12 where he stated Israel is 'becoming stronger than ever.' His office dismissed Iranian claims as 'fake news.' Iran's Tasnim News Agency (IRGC-linked, US Treasury-sanctioned) has been the primary amplifier of these rumors. However, no live, time-stamped video of Netanyahu has emerged since March 12 — a two-day gap that continues to fuel speculation.
claims.origin: Iranian state media (Tasnim News Agency), viral X/Twitter posts, AI-generated fake images. George Galloway amplified the six-fingers claim. Original six-finger screenshot posted by @gigabasedd on X (March 12).

sources.name: Snopes — Was Netanyahu killed in Iranian missile attack?
PolitiFact — Netanyahu didn't have six fingers
Lead Stories — Video does NOT show six fingers
Snopes — Six fingers AI claim analysis
Al Jazeera — Netanyahu says Israel 'stronger than ever'
Times of Israel — Netanyahu press conference
Jerusalem Post — Iran publishes false conspiracy
The Week — Israeli media responds
Sunday Guardian — Yair Netanyahu missing from public

claims.claim: Trump claims Iran is 'about to surrender'
claims.summary: No evidence of Iranian surrender. Iran's intelligence ministry is secretly contacting CIA through backchannel, but this indicates willingness to negotiate, not surrender. Iran continues offensive operations and Hormuz blockade. Trump made the claim during G7 call remarks on March 14.
claims.origin: Trump's remarks during G7 call and subsequent press availability

sources.name: Axios — Trump claims Iran is about to surrender
Al Jazeera — Day 15 live updates

claims.claim: KC-135 crash in Iraq was caused by Iranian attack
claims.summary: CENTCOM explicitly stated the KC-135 crash was not due to hostile or friendly fire. An Iranian proxy group (Kata'ib Hezbollah) claimed responsibility, but the claim is unsubstantiated. The aircraft crashed near Turaibil in western Iraq. Investigation ongoing — possible mid-air collision with a second KC-135 that landed at Ben Gurion with tail fin damage.
claims.origin: Iranian proxy group Kata'ib Hezbollah claimed responsibility on Telegram. Social media amplified the claim.

sources.name: NBC News — KC-135 crash details
CNBC — KC-135 crash Iraq
CNN — US Air Force KC-135 lost over Iraq

claims.claim: Iran claims to have hit Netanyahu's office with Khaibar missiles
claims.summary: Israel dismissed this claim as 'fake news.' No evidence of damage to the Prime Minister's office. Israel's multi-layered air defense (Iron Dome, David's Sling, Arrow) intercepted the majority of incoming missiles. IRGC-linked media amplified the claim as a propaganda victory.
claims.origin: IRGC-linked media and Iranian state television broadcast

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Day 15 updates
Jerusalem Post — Iran false conspiracy theories

claims.claim: IEA 400M barrel reserve release will crash oil prices below $80
claims.summary: Despite the largest-ever coordinated reserve release of 400M barrels (US contributing 172M from SPR), Brent crude remains above $100/barrel at $103.14. The Hormuz blockade removes ~17M barrels/day from the market, dwarfing the reserve release. Analysts say the release provides weeks of relief, not months.
claims.origin: Social media speculation after IEA announcement. Financial media amplified the expectation of a price crash.

sources.name: CNBC — IEA oil reserves, crude prices
Axios — Oil prices if Hormuz stays closed

claims.claim: 5 US refueling aircraft destroyed at a Saudi airbase
claims.summary: WSJ reported 5 US Air Force refueling aircraft were 'damaged' (not destroyed) at a Saudi airbase. The Pentagon has not officially confirmed the specific incident but acknowledged aircraft losses in the theater. The claim exaggerates 'damaged' to 'destroyed.'
claims.origin: Social media amplified a WSJ report, changing 'damaged' to 'destroyed.'

sources.name: Al Jazeera Day 15

claims.claim: White House posted Call of Duty video game footage as real war footage
claims.summary: The White House did post a video on X that merged real footage with clips from the Call of Duty video game franchise. This was widely criticized and verified by multiple fact-checking organizations. The post has since been deleted but archives exist.
claims.origin: Initially spotted by gaming communities who recognized the footage.

sources.name: Rolling Stone
CNN

claims.claim: US KC-135 Stratotanker crash killed all 6 crew members on board
claims.summary: CENTCOM confirmed the loss of a KC-135 Stratotanker refueling aircraft near Turaibil in western Iraq on March 12-13. All 6 crew members were confirmed killed. This is the 4th manned US aircraft lost since Operation Epic Fury began. A second KC-135 landed at Ben Gurion Airport with tail fin damage — possible mid-air collision under investigation.

sources.name: CNN — US Air Force KC-135 lost over Iraq
Al Jazeera — 6 US service members killed
CNBC — KC-135 crash details

claims.origin: CENTCOM official statement and multiple verified news outlets

claims.claim: UN Security Council labeled Trump a 'war criminal' after Iran strikes
claims.summary: Viral posts claimed the UN Security Council declared Trump an 'international war criminal' after the Feb. 28 strikes. Snopes rated this FALSE. While the Security Council held an emergency meeting, it issued no resolutions or statements labeling Trump a war criminal. The US is a permanent member with veto power, making such a declaration procedurally impossible. Only Iran's ambassador used the term 'war crime' in reference to the attacks.

sources.name: Snopes — Did UN label Trump a 'war criminal'?
Yahoo News — Fact Check on UN war criminal claim

claims.origin: Viral Facebook, X/Twitter, and Instagram posts

claims.claim: Trump claimed Iran has Tomahawk missiles to deflect blame for a girls' school strike
claims.summary: When asked about a Feb. 28 strike on an Iranian girls' school that killed 175 people (many children), Trump suggested Iran could have fired the Tomahawk cruise missile that hit it. PolitiFact and weapons experts confirmed Iran has no Tomahawks — only the US, UK, Japan, Australia, and Netherlands operate them. CSIS analyst Mark Cancian and Middlebury Institute's Jeffrey Lewis both confirmed Iranian cruise missiles are visually distinct from Tomahawks.

sources.name: PolitiFact — Trump blamed Iran for a strike on a girls' school
PBS — Fact-checking Trump's Tomahawk claim

claims.origin: Trump press conference on Day 10 of the war

claims.claim: Energy Secretary Chris Wright's deleted post about Hormuz tanker escort caused oil to crash 15%
claims.summary: Energy Secretary Chris Wright posted on X that the US Navy had 'successfully escorted an oil tanker through the Strait of Hormuz.' The post was deleted within 30 minutes, but not before WTI crude crashed from ~$120 to $77/barrel — a 15% drop. The White House confirmed the claim was false. Reuters reported the Navy had actually refused escort requests since the war began. Wright took 'full ownership' calling it a 'miscommunication.' Multiple analysts and Sen. Chris Murphy accused the administration of market manipulation.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — White House denies Hormuz tanker escort
Common Dreams — Trump energy chief accused of oil market manipulation
Bloomberg — Wright takes responsibility for erroneous post

claims.origin: Chris Wright's deleted X post on March 10, 2026

claims.claim: War Thunder video game footage shared as real Iran war combat — Texas Governor shared it
claims.summary: A War Thunder video game clip showing a WWII-era USS Tennessee shooting down a Messerschmitt jet was shared on X as footage of the Iran war, reaching 7.3 million views. Texas Governor Greg Abbott shared it captioned 'Bye bye' before deleting. AFP and Gaijin Entertainment (War Thunder's developer) confirmed it was game footage. The White House X account also posted Call of Duty clips mixed with real strike footage. Arma 3 gameplay was separately circulated as Iranian missile attacks.

sources.name: PC Gamer — War Thunder clip shared as Iran war footage, 7M views
Euronews — Fake videos about the Iran war

claims.origin: Video game clips posted on X/Twitter as real combat footage

claims.claim: Polymarket insider trading — new accounts made $1.2M betting on Iran strike timing
claims.summary: Six new Polymarket accounts made well-timed bets that the Iran strike would happen on Feb. 28, winning $1.2M total. Most wallets were funded within 24 hours of the attack. One account ('Magamyman') made $553K. Snopes confirmed the suspicious trading patterns but could not verify insider knowledge. Trump Jr. is a Polymarket adviser and his firm invested millions in the platform. The Trump administration dropped two federal investigations into Polymarket. $529M was traded on Iran-related contracts total.

sources.name: Snopes — Polymarket bets on Iran strikes and insider knowledge
NPR — 'Magamyman' made $553K on death of Iran's supreme leader
CNN — Iran prediction market bets scrutinized

claims.origin: Polymarket blockchain transaction analysis, Snopes investigation

claims.claim: White House press secretary did not rule out a military draft when asked directly
claims.summary: Fox Business host Maria Bartiromo asked White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt whether the US would institute a military draft. Leavitt did not rule it out, instead saying 'the president is going to do what's necessary to protect the homeland.' Snopes confirmed she did not explicitly endorse a draft but notably declined to deny one. Former Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene criticized the non-answer. A draft would require Congress to amend the Military Selective Service Act.

sources.name: Snopes — Did White House refuse to rule out a military draft?

claims.origin: Fox Business interview with Karoline Leavitt, March 12, 2026

claims.claim: Iran 'confessed' to bombing its own girls' school in Minab, killing 175 people
claims.summary: Pro-Israel social media accounts claimed Iran admitted the IRGC accidentally bombed a girls' school in Minab. This never happened — Iran blamed US-Israeli strikes. Independent investigations by the NYT, Bellingcat, CBS, and BBC Verify concluded the US was 'likely' responsible via a Tomahawk targeting error. A preliminary US military assessment also pointed to US responsibility. The original viral post on X reached 5.6 million views.

sources.name: PolitiFact — Iran did NOT take responsibility for Minab school strike
Snopes — Iran girls' school missile attack
CBS News — US assessment points to US responsibility

claims.origin: Pro-Israel X/Twitter accounts (5.6M views on original post)

claims.claim: Barron Trump bought $30-50 million in oil stocks 48 hours before the Iran strikes
claims.summary: An X post by the account 'Anon Cooker' claimed Barron Trump purchased $30-50M in oil/oil stocks 48 hours before the Feb. 28 strikes, reaching 19.8 million views. Snopes and Lead Stories found no credible evidence to support the claim — no financial records, SEC filings, or media reports corroborate it. The claim originated from a single unsourced social media post.

sources.name: Snopes — Did Barron Trump buy oil before the war?
Lead Stories — No evidence Barron Trump bought $30M in oil

claims.origin: X account 'Anon Cooker' (19.8M views), March 3, 2026

claims.claim: 'Operation Epic Fury' was named to bury 'Trump Epstein' search results
claims.summary: A viral post (1.7M views) claimed Trump insisted the war be named 'Operation Epic Fury' so that searching 'Trump Ep...' would autocomplete to 'Epic Fury' instead of 'Epstein.' Military codenames are assigned through established DoD/Joint Chiefs processes — the president does not choose them for SEO purposes. The original post was satirical but was widely shared as fact.

sources.name: National Today — Viral claim about Operation Epic Fury name

claims.origin: Satirical X post taken seriously (1.7M views)

claims.claim: AI-generated 'before/after' satellite images show total destruction of Iranian refineries
claims.summary: Multiple viral satellite images claiming to show total destruction of Iranian energy infrastructure after March 7-8 strikes were identified as AI-generated or recycled from earlier conflicts. Fact-checkers at Poynter, CNN, and India Today identified telltale AI artifacts and reverse-image-search matches to older imagery relabeled as 2026 strikes.

sources.name: Poynter — Fake videos and images fueling Iran war misinformation
CNN — Fake, AI-generated images and videos of the Iran war

claims.origin: Pro-US/Israel X/Twitter and Telegram accounts

claims.claim: Iran sank the USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier
claims.summary: AI-generated video circulating on social media claims to show the USS Abraham Lincoln sinking after an Iranian attack. PolitiFact confirmed no credible reports of the carrier being sunk, and the vessel in the video does not match real photos of the carrier. The ship's silhouette and structural details are inconsistent with Nimitz-class carriers.

sources.name: PolitiFact

claims.origin: AI-generated video first circulated on X/Twitter and Telegram, shared by accounts with 'Iran' or 'news' in usernames

claims.claim: US Delta Force operators captured by IRGC in Iran
claims.summary: AI-generated images purport to show captured US special forces operators in Iranian custody. CENTCOM explicitly denied any US forces were captured. CNN's analysis found the images show telltale signs of AI generation including distorted equipment, inconsistent uniform details, and anomalous hands/fingers.

sources.name: CNN

claims.origin: AI-generated images shared by Iranian government-linked social media accounts

claims.claim: Trump said Iran has Tomahawk missiles
claims.summary: Trump claimed in remarks that Iran possesses Tomahawk cruise missiles. This is false — Tomahawk missiles are exclusively manufactured by Raytheon for the US Navy. Iran has no Tomahawk missiles. Iran does possess indigenous cruise missile designs (e.g., Soumar, Hoveyzeh) but these are not Tomahawks.

sources.name: CNN Fact Check

claims.origin: Trump's public remarks during Iran war coverage

claims.claim: NYT published an AI-generated photo of Tehran crowds
claims.summary: Claims circulated that the New York Times published an AI-generated photo of crowds in Tehran. PolitiFact confirmed the photo was authentic — taken by a verified photojournalist. The accusation was part of a broader disinformation campaign to undermine trust in mainstream media coverage of the war.

sources.name: PolitiFact

claims.origin: Social media accounts accusing mainstream media of fabricating war imagery

claims.claim: Video shows Bahrain skyscraper destroyed by Iranian missile strike
claims.summary: A viral video claims to show a Bahraini skyscraper being destroyed by an Iranian missile. ABC News confirmed the video is AI-generated and was shared by Iranian government-linked accounts. The building depicted does not match any real structure in Bahrain, and the explosion dynamics are physically inconsistent.

sources.name: ABC News

claims.origin: AI-generated video shared by Iranian government-linked social media accounts

claims.claim: Tehran Times satellite photos show destroyed US base in Bahrain
claims.summary: Tehran Times published before/after satellite photos claiming to show a destroyed US base in Bahrain from Iranian strikes. Poynter confirmed the photos were actually from a Bahrain construction project from February 2025 — over a year before the war began. The images show demolition work, not war damage.

sources.name: Poynter

claims.origin: Tehran Times state media publication

claims.claim: US bombed Iran's oil infrastructure on Kharg Island, destroying oil exports entirely
claims.summary: Trump explicitly stated that oil infrastructure on Kharg Island was deliberately SPARED. Only military targets (army defenses, Joshen Sea Base, airport control tower, helicopter hangar) were struck. Oil facilities remain intact. Trump warned he would hit oil next only if Hormuz blockade continues.
claims.origin: Social media posts claiming oil infrastructure was destroyed went viral within minutes of the Kharg Island strike announcement.

sources.name: CNBC
The Hill

claims.claim: US Embassy in Baghdad was completely destroyed by Iranian missile
claims.summary: A missile struck the helipad inside the US Embassy compound, and a drone targeted the radar system, but the embassy was NOT destroyed. Smoke was seen rising from the compound but the structure remains intact. The IRGC claimed it as the '48th phase' of retaliation.
claims.origin: Viral social media posts and Telegram channels exaggerated the damage to the embassy.

sources.name: Jerusalem Post
Al Jazeera

claims.claim: Turkey shot down an Iranian missile — NATO is now at war with Iran
claims.summary: NATO defenses DID intercept a 3rd Iranian missile in Turkish airspace near Adana. However, Turkey has NOT invoked Article 5, and NATO is NOT at war with Iran. The interception was a defensive action. The incidents raise tensions but no formal military response has been declared by NATO.
claims.origin: Social media accounts misinterpreted the NATO missile interception as Turkey/NATO declaring war on Iran.

sources.name: Al Jazeera
Bloomberg

claims.claim: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has been killed
claims.summary: Hegseth stated Mojtaba Khamenei is 'wounded and likely disfigured' but NOT dead. JD Vance confirmed Khamenei is 'hurt' but said it's unclear if the US is responsible. The State Department's $10M bounty announcement also implies he is alive — you don't put bounties on dead people.
claims.origin: Misinterpretation of Hegseth's 'wounded and likely disfigured' comment spread as claims of his death on social media.

sources.name: Time
CNN
